title: St. Peter's Church, Royapuram
tagline: St. Peter's Church, Royapuram — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: St. Peter's Church, Royapuram, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: St. Peter's Church, Royapuram is a much-loved spiritual town in Tamil Nadu, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. Peter's Church is one of the oldest churches in Royapuram area of Chennai, the capital of the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The original structure was built in Gothic architecture in 1829 by Gurukula Vamsha Varnakula Mudaliars, a group of boatmen who were serving the East India Company. They started building the church in 1825 and consecrated it in 1829 with contributions from the Secretary of Marine Board.

topPlaces.name: Royapuram fire temple
topPlaces.description: Jal Phiroj Clubwala Dar E Meher, popularly known as the Royapuram fire temple, is a Zoroastrian fire temple at Royapuram, Chennai, India. It was built in 1910 and donated to the Madras Parsi Zarthosti Anjuman by philanthropist Phiroj M. Clubwala.

topPlaces.name: Royapuram
topPlaces.description: Royapuram is a locality in the northern part of the city of Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India. It is best known for its beach, and for Royapuram Railway Station.

topPlaces.name: Ramanujan Math Park
topPlaces.description: The Ramanujan Math Park is an Indian museum and activity center dedicated to mathematics education inside the Agastya Campus Creativity Lab located in Kuppam, in Chittoor, Andhra Pradesh. It is named after the Indian mathematician Srinivasa Ramanujan (1887–1920) who was from nearby Madras State.

topPlaces.name: Krishnaswami Temple, Muthialpet
topPlaces.description: The Krishnaswami Temple is a Hindu temple located in the neighbourhood of Muthialpet in Parry's corner (Old: George Town), Chennai, India. It was constructed in the 18th century and was the site of a major dispute between Left hand and Right hand castes in 1787 resulting in a major riot.

topPlaces.name: Old Jail Road, Chennai
topPlaces.description: Old Jail Road is one of the peripheral roads that border the historical neighbourhood and commercial centre of George Town in Chennai, India. The road marks the northern boundary of the neighbourhood and is officially divided into three, namely, from west to east, the Basin Bridge Road, the Old Jail Road, and the Ibrahim Sahib Street.

topPlaces.name: Broadway, Chennai
topPlaces.description: Broadway (officially known as Prakasam Salai, after the freedom fighter T. Prakasam) is one of the historical thoroughfares of the commercial centre of George Town in Chennai, India. The road runs north–south connecting China Bazaar Road in the south with Ibrahim Sahib Street (Old Jail Road) in the north.

topPlaces.name: M. Singaravelar Maligai
topPlaces.description: M. Singaravelar Maaligai is a state government official building that houses Chennai district Collector's office, some state Government offices and Civil and Sessions courts, in Chennai of Tamil Nadu in India. The building was named after M. Singaravelar alias Malayapuram Singaravelu, who was a social reformer.

topPlaces.name: Dharma Kidangu Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Dharma Kidangu Mosque is a mosque located in Angappa Naicken Street in the Georgetown neighbourhood of Chennai, in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. It is considered to be the oldest mosque in Muthialpet.

items.note: Jal Phiroj Clubwala Dar E Meher, popularly known as the Royapuram fire temple, is a Zoroastrian fire temple at Royapuram, Chennai, India. It was built in 1910 and donated to the Madras Parsi Zarthosti Anjuman by philanthropist Phiroj M. Clubwala.
Royapuram is a locality in the northern part of the city of Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India. It is best known for its beach, and for Royapuram Railway Station.

The Ramanujan Math Park is an Indian museum and activity center dedicated to mathematics education inside the Agastya Campus Creativity Lab located in Kuppam, in Chittoor, Andhra Pradesh. It is named after the Indian mathematician Srinivasa Ramanujan (1887–1920) who was from nearby Madras State.

The Krishnaswami Temple is a Hindu temple located in the neighbourhood of Muthialpet in Parry's corner (Old: George Town), Chennai, India. It was constructed in the 18th century and was the site of a major dispute between Left hand and Right hand castes in 1787 resulting in a major riot.

Old Jail Road is one of the peripheral roads that border the historical neighbourhood and commercial centre of George Town in Chennai, India. The road marks the northern boundary of the neighbourhood and is officially divided into three, namely, from west to east, the Basin Bridge Road, the Old Jail Road, and the Ibrahim Sahib Street.

Broadway (officially known as Prakasam Salai, after the freedom fighter T. Prakasam) is one of the historical thoroughfares of the commercial centre of George Town in Chennai, India. The road runs north–south connecting China Bazaar Road in the south with Ibrahim Sahib Street (Old Jail Road) in the north.

M. Singaravelar Maaligai is a state government official building that houses Chennai district Collector's office, some state Government offices and Civil and Sessions courts, in Chennai of Tamil Nadu in India. The building was named after M. Singaravelar alias Malayapuram Singaravelu, who was a social reformer.

The Dharma Kidangu Mosque is a mosque located in Angappa Naicken Street in the Georgetown neighbourhood of Chennai, in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. It is considered to be the oldest mosque in Muthialpet.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Peter's Church, Royapuram?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Peter's Church, Royapuram is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Peter's Church, Royapuram?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (19 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (4 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Peter's Church, Royapuram?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Peter's Church, Royapuram at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Peter's Church, Royapuram?
faq.a: Stays in St. Peter's Church, Royapuram start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Peter's Church, Royapuram?
faq.a: The top attractions are Royapuram fire temple, Royapuram, Ramanujan Math Park, Krishnaswami Temple, Muthialpet, Old Jail Road, Chennai.
